Ad Text:
AI Data Scientist

9-Month Assignment with potential to extend
Hybrid
Melbourne
Baseline or NV1
Green Light is a growing consulting organisation known for its work in Telecommunications and System Integration Services organisations. We have a requirement with our client partner for AI Data Scientist with Security Clearance to join their team on a 9-month assignment.
 
We are seeking an AI Security Engineer to build AI‑powered automation and tooling that will uplift the capability, efficiency, and effectiveness of our security engineers and analysts. This role focuses on creating new AI tools, intelligent workflows, and automations, providing a "greenfields" environment where you can help define strategy, set best practices, and build foundational AI and automation capabilities to be used within a world-leading cybersecurity operations team.
 
Key Responsibilities

Design, build, and deploy AI‑powered tools that support security engineering, SIEM workflows, and security operations processes
Develop automation for analyst workflows including: incident enrichment, log correlation, threat triage, detection tuning, and vulnerability management
Create custom LLM‑powered assistants, enrichment tools, or workflow engines to support analysts and engineers.
Build and maintain integrations with SIEM technologies (Splunk preferred), enhancing data processing, alerting, and search capabilities using AI and automation
Work with Security DevOps teams to integrate AI and automation capabilities into existing CI/CD and security tooling.
Contribute to the foundational AI automation strategy, patterns, and reusable components for the security organisation.
Drive adoption of new tools and help upskill engineers and analysts on AI‑enabled workflows.
Maintain high standards of quality, reliability, and operational robustness for automation and AI tooling.
Required Skills & Experience

Strong experience developing AI/ML‑backed tools, including LLM integrations, model‑driven automations, or AI‑assisted workflows.
Demonstrated capability in automating complex enterprise processes, using technologies like Azure Pipelines, CI/CD tooling, Python or PowerShell.
Hands‑on experience in Security DevOps / DevSecOps, including building automated security controls and integrations.
Solid understanding of core cybersecurity concepts (identity, detection engineering, vulnerability management, incident response, etc.).
Preferred Qualifications

Experience with developing secure automation pipelines, API integrations, or model-based tools.
SIEM experience, preferably with Splunk, including dashboards, searches, detection logic or data onboarding
Previous experience as a cybersecurity analyst (SOC, threat detection, incident response, or vulnerability management)
This experience helps with understanding real‑world workflows and designing tools that meaningfully support analysts.
Relevant certifications (Security, Cloud, AI/ML, or DevSecOps).
Who You Are

Excited to build new AI capabilities that meaningfully change how a security team operates.
Passionate about automation, optimisation, and tool‑building.
Comfortable exploring emerging technologies and shaping strategy in an ambiguous, greenfields environment.
A strong collaborator who can understand real analyst and engineer pain points, and build solutions that solve them.
Someone who wants ownership, influence, and the ability to make a lasting technical impact.
How to apply
Click “APPLY” or contact Shilpa at shilpac@greenlightworldwide.com or call at 0482 072 637 for a confidential discussion with Green Light Worldwide
